judge_details.reasoning: Thursday's dialogue is severely broken by a recipe continuity error — the team has been developing jalapeño corn dog bites all week, but Thursday's copy review suddenly references 'biscuit copy' and 'fat ratio explanation,' which belong to an entirely different recipe. Marcus also opens with two consecutive lines before anyone else speaks, which reads as a scripting error. The dialogue lacks any real tension, specificity to the established recipe, or meaningful character differentiation.
strengths: Margaret's voice remains clipped and directive, consistent with prior days
The structure of a copy review session is a plausible next step after locking the hero shot
issues: Critical hallucination/continuity break: 'biscuit copy' and 'fat ratio' have no connection to jalapeño corn dog bites established across Monday-Wednesday
Marcus opens with two back-to-back lines with no intervening dialogue, suggesting a scripting error
No reference to the locked cornmeal ratio (18%), the bite-through hero shot, or any prior week's work
Dialogue is extremely thin — no actual copy is shared, no real debate occurs, and the day closes abruptly with no earned resolution
